name: 30_Image_Segmentation_UNet_PascalVOC_DL
query: Je, unaweza kunisaidia kuanzisha mradi wa kugawanya picha kwa kutumia seti ya data ya Pascal VOC na modeli ya U-Net iliyofunzwa awali iliyotekelezwa katika PyTorch? Hakuna haja ya mafunzo ya ziada. Tumia uboreshaji wa data (mfano, kugeuza na kuzungusha picha), tumia kipimo cha Dice kwa tathmini, hifadhi picha zilizogawanywa kwenye `results/figures/`, tengeneza na uhifadhi uhuishaji wa GIF unaoonyesha picha kabla na baada ya kugawanya kwenye `results/figures/segmentation_results.gif`, na unda Jupyter Notebook iliyo na maelezo mazuri ya usanifu wa modeli, mchakato, na matokeo ya kugawanya, ukibadilisha kuwa ripoti ya HTML (ukiihifadhi kama `results/report.html`).

requirements.criteria: Seti ya data ya "Pascal VOC" inatumika katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Uboreshaji wa data, ikiwa ni pamoja na kugeuza na kuzungusha picha, unafanywa katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Modeli ya "U-Net" iliyofunzwa awali kutoka PyTorch inatumika bila mafunzo ya ziada na imehifadhiwa katika `models/saved_models/`.
Kipimo cha "Dice coefficient" kinatumika kwa tathmini na kinapaswa kuhifadhiwa katika `results/metrics/`.
Picha zilizogawanywa zinahifadhiwa kwenye folda iliyotajwa `results/figures/`.
Uhuishaji wa GIF wa picha kabla na baada ya kugawanya unazalishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/segmentation_results.gif`.

Jupyter Notebook inaundwa ikijumuisha usanifu wa modeli, mchakato wa kutumia modeli iliyofunzwa awali, na matokeo ya kugawanya. Inabadilishwa kuwa ripoti ya HTML na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/report.html`.

preferences.criteria: Jupyter Notebook inapaswa kujumuisha vipande vya msimbo vilivyo na maelezo mazuri vinavyoelezea kila hatua ya mchakato.
Uhuishaji wa GIF unapaswa kuonyesha wazi mabadiliko kabla na baada ya kugawanya kwenye picha tofauti kutoka seti ya data.

judge_stats.criteria: Seti ya data ya "Pascal VOC" inatumika katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` only mentions `"Pascal VOC"` in the `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` string and `KEYWORDS = ["Pascal VOC"]`, but the actual loader `load_dataset()` is just a placeholder returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`. There is no code importing, configuring, or loading the Pascal VOC dataset.

judge_stats.criteria: Uboreshaji wa data, ikiwa ni pamoja na kugeuza na kuzungusha picha, unafanywa katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` only contains a placeholder `build_transforms()` that returns `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. There is no actual augmentation implementation, and flipping is not present at all. The file also explicitly labels this as a “placeholder,” so evidence for performing both flipping and rotating images is missing.

judge_stats.criteria: Modeli ya "U-Net" iliyofunzwa awali kutoka PyTorch inatumika bila mafunzo ya ziada na imehifadhiwa katika `models/saved_models/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> There is no evidence that a pre-trained PyTorch U-Net is used, run without additional training, or saved in `models/saved_models/`. The only provided source file, `src/data_loader.py`, contains just a minimal placeholder loader and transform stub, with no U-Net model code, no PyTorch model loading logic, no inference-only workflow, and no save operation. The trajectory evidence also explicitly states there is no direct confirmation of loading a pre-trained U-Net or saving a model in `models/saved_models/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> There is no evidence that the Dice coefficient is computed or saved. The workspace shows `results/metrics/` exists, but no metric files are listed inside it, and the only provided source file `src/data_loader.py` contains only dataset/loading placeholders and no evaluation logic for Dice coefficient. The trajectory evidence also explicitly states there is no shown reference to Dice coefficient usage or saving in `results/metrics/`.

reason: <SATISFIED> The workspace shows `results/figures/segmentation_results.gif`, which is direct evidence that segmented image output was saved in the specified `results/figures/` folder. The trajectory evidence also states that image segmentation outputs were directed to `results/figures/` with no reported write errors.

reason: <SATISFIED> The workspace contains `results/figures/segmentation_results.gif`, and the trajectory evidence states the final relevant step was saving the GIF after generating a before/after segmentation animation, with no reported errors or warnings.
